commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the code repair must be narrowed and the external PR still needs real behavior proof from a live setup.; Needs attention: The diff broadens externally installed plugin runtime execution at gateway startup, which is a plugin trust-boundary concern.; Review finding: [P1] Narrow the enabled-plugin startup fallback — src/plugins/gateway-startup-plugin-ids.ts:2128... commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a gateway startup fallback that loads non-bundled plugins when their effective activation state is explicitly enabled, and updates startup tests to expect those plugins to load.

PR surface: Source +55, Tests 0. Total +55 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main derives gateway runtime loading from `startup.pluginIds`, and a plugin-entry-only external channel does not match the configured-channel predicate. This review did not run the reported macOS/Homebrew live setup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the code repair must be narrowed and the external PR still needs real behavior proof from a live setup.

target.title: fix(plugins): load explicitly enabled externally-installed plugins at gateway startup
target.body:
## Summary

- Externally-installed npm plugins enabled via `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled=true` were never loaded at gateway startup if they didn't also match a channel, provider, hook, harness, or `activation.onStartup` predicate.
- Root cause: `resolveGatewayStartupPluginPlanFromRegistry` only checked specific manifest-based predicates and had no fallback for plugins explicitly enabled by the user but outside those categories.
- Adds `canStartExplicitlyEnabledPlugin` predicate following the same guard pattern as `canStartTrustedToolPolicyPlugin`, inserted after all specific predicates but before the memory/dreaming fallback.
- Fixes #93219

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts` — 144/144 tests pass
- `pnpm test src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts src/plugins/config-state.test.ts src/plugins/config-contracts.test.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.test.ts src/plugins/discovery.test.ts` — 342/342 tests pass
- `openclaw-pr-review` — clean, 0 accepted findings, best-fix confirmed
- CI: pending

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Externally-installed npm plugins were silently skipped during gateway startup if they didn't match any built-in channel/provider/hook/harness predicate
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-93219-gateway-external-plugin-load` at `6d7c5c7b3`
- **Tests**: 144 channel-plugin-ids tests pass, 3 test cases updated to expect explicitly enabled plugins to load
- **Evidence after fix**: `canStartExplicitlyEnabledPlugin` predicate catches non-bundled plugins with `enabled=true` in the registry activation state
- **What was not tested**: Full integration test with a real externally-installed npm plugin in a live gateway (requires macOS + WeChat setup)
target.author: xialonglee
